en_definition: to register officially as a student at a university or course
example1: You must enroll in courses before the deadline.
example1_ru: Вы должны записаться на курсы до истечения крайнего срока.
example2: Students enroll in the program during the registration period.

en_definition: the complete list of subjects or courses offered by a school
example1: The curriculum includes chemistry, biology, and physics.
example1_ru: Учебный план включает химию, биологию и физику.
example2: Review the curriculum to choose your courses wisely.

en_definition: a department of a university or the teachers who work there
example1: She works in the Faculty of Science at the university.
example1_ru: Она работает на факультете естественных наук в университете.
example2: The faculty members hold office hours for students.

en_definition: money given to a student to help pay for education
example1: She received a scholarship to study at the university.
example1_ru: Она получила стипендию на обучение в университете.
example2: Apply for scholarships to reduce your tuition costs.

en_definition: the money paid for education at a school or university
example1: International tuition is higher than domestic student rates.
example1_ru: Плата за обучение для иностранных студентов выше, чем для местных.
example2: The tuition must be paid before classes begin.

en_definition: a building at a school or university where students live
example1: First-year students usually live together in a dormitory.
example1_ru: Первокурсники обычно живут вместе в общежитии.
example2: Her dormitory room overlooks the main university library.

en_definition: an official document showing you have completed a course
example1: You will receive a certificate upon course completion.
example1_ru: По окончании курса вы получите сертификат.
example2: The certificate is valid and recognized internationally.

en_definition: a condition that must be agreed to as part of a contract or plan
example1: The contract includes a stipulation about the delivery deadline.
example1_ru: В контракте есть условие о сроках поставки.
example2: One stipulation requires all applicants to submit references.

en_definition: having the qualifications needed for something
example1: You are eligible to apply for the exchange program.
example1_ru: Вы имеете право подать заявку на программу обмена.
example2: Students must be eligible to receive financial aid.

en_definition: required by law or rule; you must do it
example1: Attendance at lectures is compulsory for this course.
example1_ru: Посещение лекций по этому курсу обязательно.
example2: Compulsory modules must be completed for graduation.

en_definition: something you can choose to do but do not have to
example1: These courses are optional but recommended for your degree.
example1_ru: Эти курсы необязательны, но рекомендуются для получения вашей степени.
example2: Lab attendance is optional but highly advised.

en_definition: a rule made by an organization or local authority to govern its own affairs
example1: The new bylaw requires all bikes to be registered with the city.
example1_ru: Новое постановление требует регистрации всех велосипедов в городе.
example2: Committee members voted to update an outdated bylaw last spring.

en_definition: to formally request a decision be changed or reconsidered
example1: You can appeal your grade if you disagree with it.
example1_ru: Вы можете обжаловать свою оценку, если не согласны с ней.
example2: Students may appeal disciplinary decisions to the committee.

en_definition: to inform someone officially about something important
example1: We will notify you of your admission decision by email.
example1_ru: Мы уведомим вас о решении по зачислению по электронной почте.
example2: The university will notify students about changes.

en_definition: to check that specific facts or information are accurate and consistent
example1: The office called to corroborate details before approving the form.
example1_ru: Из офиса позвонили, чтобы уточнить детали перед утверждением формы.
example2: Staff must corroborate details with the applicant's official documents.

en_definition: to arrange for something to happen at a different time than originally planned
example1: The office had to reschedule the meeting due to a conflict.
example1_ru: Офису пришлось перенести встречу из-за накладки в расписании.
example2: Please call us early if you need to reschedule your appointment.

en_definition: to officially cancel a law, order, or agreement
example1: The university decided to rescind the policy after complaints.
example1_ru: Университет решил отменить эту политику после жалоб.
example2: Officials chose to rescind the permit following the investigation.

en_definition: to formally send in an official application or petition
example1: Students must file a request for an extension in writing.
example1_ru: Студенты обязаны подать заявку на продление в письменной форме.
example2: She had to file a request before the office deadline.

en_definition: extra time allowed to complete a task or assignment
example1: Request an extension if you need more time for your essay.
example1_ru: Запросите продление срока, если вам нужно больше времени на эссе.
example2: The professor may grant an extension for valid reasons.

en_definition: using someone else's work as your own without permission
example1: Plagiarism will result in serious academic penalties.
example1_ru: Плагиат повлечёт за собой серьёзные академические санкции.
example2: The university has strict policies against plagiarism.

en_definition: to check information in one place against related information elsewhere
example1: Librarians cross-reference sources carefully before confirming a citation is accurate.
example1_ru: Библиотекари тщательно сверяют источники по перекрёстным ссылкам, прежде чем подтвердить точность цитаты.
example2: The auditor will cross-reference receipts with the bank statement.

en_definition: the practice of quoting or referencing an author's work
example1: Each citation must follow the required format exactly.
example1_ru: Каждая ссылка должна точно соответствовать требуемому формату.
example2: Proper citation prevents plagiarism accusations.

en_definition: a division of a university specializing in a subject
example1: The Chemistry Department is located on the ground floor.
example1_ru: Кафедра химии находится на первом этаже.
example2: Contact the department if you have questions.

en_definition: a teacher at a university who gives lectures
example1: The lecturer explained the concept clearly in class.
example1_ru: Преподаватель ясно объяснил понятие на занятии.
example2: Ask your lecturer for clarification on the assignment.

en_definition: a person who oversees or directs student work or research
example1: Your supervisor will provide feedback on your project.
example1_ru: Ваш научный руководитель даст отзыв по вашему проекту.
example2: Meet with your supervisor every two weeks.

en_definition: a teacher who provides individual or small-group instruction
example1: Your personal tutor helps you with academic and personal issues.
example1_ru: Ваш личный куратор помогает вам с академическими и личными вопросами.
example2: Hire a tutor if you need help with specific subjects.

en_definition: a test or evaluation used to measure student knowledge
example1: Continuous assessment accounts for twenty percent of the grade.
example1_ru: Текущая оценка составляет двадцать процентов от итоговой отметки.
example2: The final assessment consists of an exam and project.

en_definition: a mark showing how well a student performed on a test
example1: Your grade depends on your exam score and assignments.
example1_ru: Ваша оценка зависит от результата экзамена и заданий.
example2: Check your grades on the university portal.

en_definition: an official record of a student's grades and courses
example1: Request an official transcript for your job application.
example1_ru: Запросите официальную выписку из зачётной книжки для заявления о приёме на работу.
example2: Your transcript will be sent to the employer.

en_definition: an official document listing a student's courses and grades
example1: The office mailed her transcript record to the new university.
example1_ru: Деканат отправил её выписку успеваемости в новый университет по почте.
example2: You will need a transcript record to apply for the scholarship.

en_definition: a secondary subject a student studies alongside their main area of study
example1: She chose economics as her minor field alongside chemistry.
example1_ru: Она выбрала экономику своей дополнительной специализацией наряду с химией.
example2: Choosing a minor field can broaden your career options.

en_definition: a secondary subject of study in university degree
example1: Adding a minor requires only a few additional courses.
example1_ru: Добавление дополнительной специализации требует лишь нескольких дополнительных курсов.
example2: She completed a minor in Mathematics alongside her degree.

en_definition: a course that students can choose to take but do not have to
example1: You must choose two electives from the list provided.
example1_ru: Вы должны выбрать два курса по выбору из предложенного списка.
example2: Electives let you study topics that interest you.

en_definition: the conditions a candidate must meet to be accepted into a program
example1: The admission criteria include grades and a personal essay.
example1_ru: Критерии зачисления включают оценки и личное эссе.
example2: Each university sets its own admission criteria for applicants.

en_definition: a small class where students discuss and exchange ideas
example1: Attend the weekly seminar to discuss research findings.
example1_ru: Посещайте еженедельный семинар для обсуждения результатов исследований.
example2: Seminars encourage active participation from all students.

en_definition: a room equipped for scientific experiments and research
example1: Laboratory work is part of the course requirement.
example1_ru: Лабораторная работа является частью требований курса.
example2: Conduct your experiment in the chemistry laboratory.

en_definition: a long academic essay presenting original research
example1: Write a thesis on a topic that interests you.
example1_ru: Напишите дипломную работу на тему, которая вам интересна.
example2: Your thesis must demonstrate original contribution.

en_definition: a long written research project for a doctoral degree
example1: Complete your dissertation before applying for graduation.
example1_ru: Завершите диссертацию перед подачей заявления на выпуск.
example2: The dissertation must be peer-reviewed and approved.

en_definition: careful study or investigation to discover new knowledge
example1: Conduct research to support your findings.
example1_ru: Проведите исследование, чтобы подкрепить свои выводы.
example2: The university supports student research projects.

en_definition: a written work that is printed and distributed publicly
example1: Submit your research for publication in a journal.
example1_ru: Отправьте своё исследование на публикацию в журнал.
example2: Her publications demonstrate expertise in the field.

en_definition: a periodical publication containing articles on academic topics
example1: Read peer-reviewed articles from academic journals.
example1_ru: Читайте рецензируемые статьи из научных журналов.
example2: Publish your findings in a reputable journal.

en_definition: evaluation of work by experts in the same field
example1: The paper will undergo peer review before publication.
example1_ru: Статья пройдёт рецензирование перед публикацией.
example2: Peer review ensures quality of academic research.

en_definition: relating to education or scholarly study
example1: Academic performance is important for scholarships.
example1_ru: Академическая успеваемость важна для получения стипендий.
example2: Use academic sources for your research paper.

en_definition: the period during which schools and universities operate
example1: The academic year typically starts in September.
example1_ru: Учебный год обычно начинается в сентябре.
example2: The academic year is divided into two semesters.

en_definition: a fixed period of time during which classes are held at a school
example1: Tuition is due at the start of every academic term.
example1_ru: Плата за обучение вносится в начале каждого учебного семестра.
example2: Students choose their courses before each academic term begins.

en_definition: a fixed period of study in a school year
example1: The autumn term runs from September to December.
example1_ru: Осенний триместр длится с сентября по декабрь.
example2: Mid-term exams are scheduled for next week.

en_definition: a smaller classroom used for discussion-based university classes
example1: The professor moved the class to a larger seminar room.
example1_ru: Профессор перевёл занятие в аудиторию побольше.
example2: Our seminar room only holds about twenty students.

ru_translation: индивидуальное занятие, занятие в малой группе
en_definition: a small class with individual instruction on a topic
example1: Your tutorial group meets once a week.
example1_ru: Ваша группа для занятий встречается раз в неделю.
example2: Get help with difficult concepts in tutorial sessions.

en_definition: the final date by which something must be completed or submitted
example1: Applications received after the cutoff date are not accepted.
example1_ru: Заявки, поданные после крайнего срока, не принимаются.
example2: The registrar announced a new cutoff date for late fees.

en_definition: a spoken test used to evaluate a student's knowledge or skills
example1: Every graduate must pass an oral assessment before defending.
example1_ru: Каждый выпускник должен сдать устный экзамен перед защитой.
example2: She practiced for weeks before her oral assessment in French.
